name: React Testing
description: Best practices for testing React components with Testing Library and Jest/Vitest
author: StartUpp AI IDE
rules: Use React Testing Library for component tests, never Enzyme
Test user behavior and interactions, not implementation details
Use screen.getByRole, screen.getByText for queries -- avoid getByTestId unless necessary
Mock external dependencies (API calls, modules) but never mock React internals
Write integration tests that cover full user flows, not just unit tests
Use userEvent over fireEvent for realistic interaction simulation
Assert on visible output, not internal state

promptTemplates.name: Test Component
promptTemplates.description: Generate tests for a React component
promptTemplates.template: Write comprehensive tests for the {{componentName}} component. Cover: rendering, user interactions, edge cases, and error states. Use React Testing Library and {{testRunner}}.

promptTemplates.name: Test Custom Hook
promptTemplates.description: Generate tests for a React custom hook
promptTemplates.template: Write tests for the {{hookName}} custom hook using @testing-library/react-hooks renderHook. Test all return values, state changes, and edge cases.

conventions:
Testing conventions:
- Test files live next to the component: Component.test.tsx
- Use describe/it blocks for organization
- Setup shared rendering in beforeEach when appropriate
- Clean up after tests that modify global state
- Name test files matching the component: MyComponent.test.tsx
- Group related assertions in the same it() block when they test the same behavior
